item.title: FRYday Night!
item.description:
Friday, May 9, 2025 at 4 PM

Location: Location ID: m729srp9

FRY-nally. An event you can put ketchup on! It’s FRYday Night… and we’re kicking off the weekend AND Seattle Beer Week with FREE Fries for the first 100 people who buy a pint of the official beer of Seattle Beer Week! Even better, Plus, FREE SBW T-Shirt Giveaways, Live Music, FREE Treats, Special Appearances and more!

From Seattle Beer Week

item.title: Spring Beer Fest
item.description:
Friday, May 9, 2025 at 4:01 PM

Location: Location ID: mabn0quq

This mini beer festival celebrates our new spring seasonal releases, as well as a kind of retrospective on some of our favorite releases of the last couple of months. All rounded out with some great guest taps from a bunch of great local producers and friends.Our Spring Beer Fest will include 12 cask ales, including our Spring seasonal favorite Stinging Nettle Ale and a new version of an old Machine House classic, Oyster Stout. We will also have casks of our collaboration beers with Fast Fashion, Greyscale Brewing and Lowercase (the last ever cask of London Pilsner!).Plus several excellent guest beers from Structures Brewing, Human People, E9, Bizarre Brewing, and ciders from Alpenfire, Woodinville and Yonder to round out our selection.Come on by throughout the weekend to sample through everything.Spring Beer Fest is May 9-11 at Machine House in Hillman City. Perfect timing for Seattle Beer Week, and Mothers Day! This event is free to enter and open to all ages.

From Seattle Beer Week

Saturday, May 10, 2025 at 11 AM

Location: Location ID: m992rk4x

item.title: Dirty Couch Brewing Anniversary Party
item.description:
Saturday, May 10, 2025 at 12 PM

Location: Location ID: ma5g4qym

Dirty Couch Brewing is celebrating our 10th anniversary and kicking off Seattle Beer Week! Come to celebrate with us at our Magnolia taproom on May 10. We will have new releases, a pig roast, and live music from our friends, Grunge Tree.Festivities happening all day with food starting around 3 pm and music at 6:30 pm.Dirty Couch Brewing is family and dog-friendly brewery specializing in unique barrel aged sours, while crafting more classic beer styles. We have a beer for every palate!

From Seattle Beer Week

item.title: Flying Bike Coop Member for a Day
item.description:
Saturday, May 10, 2025 at 1 PM

Location: Location ID: m9n0qquy

Ever wondered what it’s like to be part of a beer-loving, community-powered co-op? Now’s your chance to find out.Join us for Flying Bike Co-op Member for a Day — an exclusive event where you get a taste of what it's like to own a part of Washington's first co-op brewery. Whether you’re a die-hard hop head or just co-op curious, this is your opportunity to sip, socialize, and see what membership is all about — no commitment required.Expect fresh pours, good vibes, and even better beerSaturday 5/10 and Tuesday 5/13Come thirsty, leave inspired — and maybe even as our newest co-op member. Cheers to community, craft, and cold ones!

From Seattle Beer Week

item.title: Pine Box Beer Can Derby
item.description:
Saturday, May 10, 2025 at 3 PM

Location: Location ID: m7279q0m

The race is on! Come & try your luck racing cans cub scout style down the tracks to beer glory. Join us at 3pm to purchase a beer, canned of course, & a race kit. We’ll race in heats starting at 5pm w/ prizes for 1st, 2nd, & 3rd + most attractive car! Tools will be provided. Cub scout gear encouraged! See you at the starting line.3pm to 5pm - Build Time 5pm - RACE TIME!!ALL CARS MUST PASS INSPECTION TO QUALIFY FOR THE RACE Following are the inspection points 1. The can weight shall not exceed 5.0 ounces. The official race scale shall be considered final. 2. The overall length of the can shall not exceed 7 inches. 3. The overall width of the can shall not exceed 2 ¾ inches. 4. The can must have 1 ¾” clearance between the wheels. 5. The can must have 3/8” clearance underneath the body. 6. The can may be shaped any way that is desired. 7. The can must be freewheeling, with no starting devices. 8. You must use official Pine Box Wheels and Axles, YOUR CAN CAR MUST BE BUILT ON SITE.Each can must pass inspection by the official inspection committee before it will be allowed to compete. The Inspection Committee has the responsibility to disqualify those cans that do not meet these specifications.Hosted by New Belgium Brewing

From Seattle Beer Week

item.title: Gate City IPA Trivia Night
item.description:
Tuesday, May 13, 2025 at 7 PM

Location: Location ID: m992z2fo

Join us for this Seattle Beer Week 16 event! Two rounds of trivia at 7pm and 8pm and delicious hazy IPA for a great cause. Free to play. CALL AHEAD TO RESERVE YOUR SPOT - 206.729.7433With every pint and pitcher sold, a donation is made to North Helpline, helping to keep food on the table and a roof overhead for our neighbors. Drinking a great IPA counts as a good deed for the day!About Gate City Community Benefit:Hellbent Brewing, Watershed Pub & Kitchen, Project 9 Brewing, and local community partners have come together again to organize the 8th annual Seattle Beer Week event that features Hellbent Brewing’s award-winning Gate City IPA. A combination of the two Seattle neighborhoods, Northgate and Lake City, this brew celebrates the North Seattle community by raising funds for North Helpline.Meet with North Helpline staff, win fun prizes, and enjoy Gate City IPA throughout Seattle Beer Week! Stay tuned for more details.

item.title: 5th Annual SBW CORNHOLE SHOWDOWN
item.description:
Wednesday, May 14, 2025 at 6 PM

Location: Location ID: m9iqgaee

Join your friends and coworkers here for a raucous, good time WED 5/14. 6-11pm. If you were here last year...well, you know you had fun. Our 60 taps (and clean lines) are focused on Seattle & WA-based breweries and cider makers; typically you can expect 42 local kegs on tap and others waiting on deck (chances are draft from your favorite Seattle breweries). Free live music 7-9pm, good food and the annual, double elimination, SBW Corn Hole Tournament that starts off friendly enough ~ then tends to get serious. How it works is a Rep/Owner/Brewer from each brewery on hand is paired with a non-beer-biz person. Swag prizes abound. We have a lot of space and lots of boards, so typically everybody gets to play. But if you 100% want to play, show up closer to 5pm (and/or email Kelly@theGrowlerGuys.com).So, come throw some bags, drink some great Seattle-based beer, chum up with industry friends you may already know, and just hang out for a few hours with other craft-loving guests. It's an Open Party. Feel free to invite coworkers & friends. Great beer, food, corn hole, live music, parking ~ I feel an Underberg coming on...

From Seattle Beer Week

item.title: Tour De Pints
item.description:
Saturday, May 17, 2025 at 11 AM

Location: Location ID: m9n0qquy

The Tour de Pints is an annual bike ride throughout Seattle to visit a bunch of breweries and drink a bunch of beer! Can't beat that!The full route has not been announced yet, but you can expect somewhere around 25 miles of bike path, bike lane, and safe road riding that will take you to approximately five breweries. The route will end in Ballard, not too far from the start point. The ride is unsupported, but there will be plenty of food options around to keep you fueled up along the way.At each stop, there will be raffles for prizes, as if you need an excuse to ride bikes and drink beers!The event is totally free to attend, though donations are accepted by purchasing a donation ticket. No purchase required. Drinking beer is not required, or even advisable. Know your limits!The route will be revealed as the event draws nearer!RSVP Here: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/tour-de-pints-2025-tickets-1317605195609

From Seattle Beer Week

item.title: Coworking Open House Week @ The Cloud Room
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Cloud Room, 1424 11th Ave Suite 400, Seattle, WA

Curious about coworking and looking for the perfect spot to get work done and reach your goals? Join us for a complimentary day during our upcoming Open House Week and experience what it’s like to be a part of our incredible community! What's Included: One Free Trial Day: Pick any day during Open House Week to try out our coworking space, free of charge! Arrive anytime 9am or later — whatever works best with your schedule. Access to our beautiful workspace including quiet desk space with monitors, large lounge area, phone booths, bar, and outdoor patio. Complimentary coffee, tea, snacks and kombucha. High-speed Wi-Fi, printing, and meeting rooms available for rent The chance to meet like-minded professionals and get a feel for the Cloud Room vibe. Special Offer: If you love your free day with us and decide to become a member, we’ll waive your initiation fees when you sign up! How to Join: Reserve your free trial day by selecting one day of your choice on our Calendly \(one free trial day per person\) Show up and enjoy your day at Cloud Room! Be sure to check in with the staff at the front of the lounge so we can give you a quick tour of the space and get you set up with the wifi. Sign up for membership to take advantage of waived initiation fees. Don’t miss this chance to test out our space, meet amazing people, and see if Cloud Room is the perfect fit for your work needs! Current Promotions: ⭐️ Sign up for a grounded membership and get your first month free \+ 10% off you first year of membership. 3 month commitment required. ⭐️ Floating Membership Promo: Take 50% off your first full month of floating membership. Spaces are limited—RSVP today.

From SEAtoday - Food & Drink

item.title: Hill Talk: Stoup Brewing Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Stoup Brewing, 1158 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Description HillTalk at Stoup Brewing \(Capitol Hill\) Tuesday, April 14  4:00-6:00 PM Join GSBA for our next HillTalk as we gather at Stoup Brewing's Capitol Hill taproom for a candid conversation about what's happening in our neighborhood and what it means for local businesses. HillTalk brings together GSBA members, business owners, community partners, and neighborhood leaders for real dialogue and practical updates. Whether you're navigating public safety concerns, city policy changes, foot traffic shifts, or new opportunities for collaboration, this is your space to stay informed and connected. Location: Stoup Brewing - Capitol Hill What is HillTalk? HillTalk is a casual, community-centered gathering where Capitol Hill businesses and community members can hear timely updates, ask questions, share perspective, and connect with the people shaping our neighborhood. What to Expect Neighborhood updates impacting Capitol Hill businesses Connection with fellow business owners and community partners A welcoming, relaxed setting to build relationships and stay engaged Come As You Are Grab a pint, pull up a chair, and be part of the conversation shaping Capitol Hill's future.

From SEAtoday - Food & Drink

Current Promotions: ⭐️ Sign up for a grounded membership and get your first month free \+ 10% off you first year of membership. 3 month commitment required. ⭐️ Floating Membership Promo: Take 50% off your first full month of floating membership. Spaces are limited—RSVP today.

item.title: GU Earth Day Volunteering
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Duthie Hill Mountain Bike Park, Issaquah, WA

Zags—In honor of Earth Day, let’s get outside and give back! Join the Seattle Chapter for a community service day at Duthie Hill Mountain Bike Park in Sammamish on April 18, 10 AM–2 PM. We’ll be teaming up with Evergreen Mountain Bike Alliance to do trail maintenance at one of the region’s most-loved riding spots. No experience needed—just bring your energy, your Zag spirit, and a pair of work gloves. All tools will be provided by Evergreen, and we’ll have pizza and drinks ready for everyone. Kids are welcomed! Spots are limited—sign up here: https://www.evergreenmtb.org/index.php?option=com\_civicrm&task=civicrm/event/info&reset=1&id=5306 Let’s make an impact together. Go Zags!

item.title: Bartender's Circle Summit 2026 - Seattle Cocktail Week
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: PACIFIC PLACE, 600 Pine Street, Seattle, WA

The world’s bar community is converging on Seattle. For three days, we are transforming the iconic Pacific Place into a multi-floor global campus for the hospitality elite. This isn’t just a regional seminar—it’s where the industry meets to define the next era of cocktail culture.Whether you are traveling from across the ocean or across the city, the Summit offers a masterclass in professional evolution, led by the most influential voices in the game.️ Learn From the LegendsWe’ve curated an unparalleled roster of international talent. Join these icons for deep-dives into the craft:Ivy Mix: Global bar owner and co-founder of Speed Rack.Jeffrey Morgenthaler: Renowned author and master of modern bar management.Camper English: The world’s leading "cocktail scientist" and expert on spirits physics.Jim Romdall: Seattle’s industry veteran on sustainability and long-term bar business....plus dozens more global ambassadors and master distillers. Three Tracks to MasteryOur curriculum is strategically divided into three pillars of excellence, allowing you to customize your education based on your career goals:The Skills Track: Precision meets innovation. These hands-on labs and technical sessions focus on the "how" behind world-class drinks—covering everything from advanced ice programs and molecular science to creative menu development and speed of service.The Spirits Track: This is for the true student of the glass. Go deep into the technical heritage of global categories like Cognac and Pisco, explore distillation secrets with master producers, and refine your palate with curated tastings of rare expressions and new releases.The Business Track: Build a career that lasts. These sessions tackle the "why" and "how much" of the industry—focusing on bar operations, sustainable business models, hospitality leadership, and maintaining physical and mental wellness for a long-term career.️ Secure Your Global AccessThe Summit Class Pass - $45The International Professional’s Choice Full 3-day access to all educational tracks \(Skills, Spirits, Business\) at Pacific Place. Includes entry to exclusive industry lounges and global networking sessions.The Pro-Bundle \(Summit \+ Carnival of Cocktails\) - $75The Ultimate Experience | Best Value Maximize your trip to Seattle. This combo deal is for those who want the full educational foundation plus the high-energy celebration of the Saturday Carnival.Full 3-Day Summit Access: Every class, every speaker, every track.Saturday Carnival Entry: The PNW’s largest spirits tasting event at Pacific Place.12 Tasting Tokens: Sample 100\+ brands and immersive pop-ups.The Bundle Discount: Get the best industry rate by pairing your education and your celebration.

From SEAtoday - Food & Drink

item.title: Paella Pop-up at a/stir
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: a/stir, 818 E Pike St, Seattle, WA

You know us \(or maybe you don't\). We are obssessed with Northern Mediterranean cuisine. What is more quintessential than Paella when it comes to Northern Mediterranean? \(Well, a few other dishes but paella is certainly one of them\). So, join us for a very limited pop-up for a seafood paella pop-up at a/stir. The ONLY option for dinner is, you guessed it, paella. What is in it? This one will feature smoked chicken thighs, mussels, shrimp and chorizo \(no, we won't forget the saffron, onion and the bell peppers\). No vegan or vegetarian options this time around. What else comes with it? A glass of Spanish wine - white or red, your choice. Anything else? Well, if you so choose, you will be able to order a side salad and a basque cheesecake as dessert \(neither are included in price, and both in very limited quantities\). Seatings at 6pm, 7pm or 8pm. Choose carefully. :-\) Price? $50/person, inclusive of taxes and gratuity. Includes a generous portion of paella and a 5 oz glass of delicious Spanish wine. Salad and Basque cheesecake optionally available at the restaurant at time of dinner. FINE PRINT Since this is a pop-up, we cannot make any accomodations and substitutions with food content. There will NOT be any other regular menu items on the day of service - this is a pop-up for PAELLA only. Not a drinker? Don't wanna pay for the wine? Consider joining us at another pop-up event. Since this is such a limited and narrow scope engagement, we are not in a position to accomodate requests.

From SEAtoday - Food & Drink

White – Accelerated Results   Topic: My 24/7 Ai Assistant 11:15am – 11:45am – Open Networking // Business Showcase Tables 11:45am – Food Ready 12pm – Meeting Starts 1pm – Meeting Ends Member – $15 pre-pay or $20 the day of. Price is only $30 and you’ll receive table space to showcase yourself and your business before, during, and after our scheduled Lunch & Learn meeting. We appreciate your support and know you will make lots of connections. More details will be sent to you once you submit your application. Payment will be required once your application is approved and your showcase space is reserved. Business Showcase Application available here!   Member of our Chamber? item.title: Tyler McGinnis @ Perihelion Brewery
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Perihelion Brewery, 2800 16th Ave South, Seattle, WA 98144

Born and raised in Los Angeles, CA It was not until after graduating high school and moving to Fremont, NE, to attend Midland University on scholarship for football that Tyler began to pursue his passion for music with vigor. After graduating, he relocated to Seattle, WA in the summer of 2017, where he currently resides and has been making impressive leaps and bounds in his songwriting, performing, and overall musical identity during his time in the PNW. His creative spin on past and present experiences breeds songs that lyrically offer a sensationalized window into his life. Tyler boasts powerful vocal performances that mix rich styles of Americana and Alternative Country music with Southern Rock and a fresh twist of soulful melodies. His music will have you singing along to a good time drinking song, reminiscing about long lost love and the regretful or questionable choices of your past, then leave you feeling uplifted, refreshed, and in an all around good mood.

From Perihelion Brewery

item.title: April Business After Hours
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: the Neighborhood, 6503 California Ave SW, Seattle, WA

We're feeling neighborly at April's Business After Hours at The Neighborhood! This Morgan Junction restaurant is built on the promise that guests should feel as cozy here as they do in their own home. This community driven spot focuses on great tasting food and beverages, exceptional service, and genuine hospitality, with an approach rooted in quality, consistency, and connection that has earned it a loyal local following along California Avenue. In person networking is more effective because shared space and real time interaction increase recall and follow up, a finding supported by Harvard Business Review research showing face to face meetings strengthen professional relationships more than digital alternatives. This evening is meant to feel easy and social in the same way the restaurant does, with room for genuine conversation and connections that make sense locally. Date and Time Wednesday Apr 22 5:30 PM - 7:30 PM Location 6503 California Ave SW Seattle WA 98136

item.title: Coworkers in the Vineyard Lay Ministry Convocation
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Holy Family Parish, Kirkland, WA

Lay ministers in the Archdiocese of Seattle are welcome to join us for the annual Coworkers in the Vineyard Lay Ministry Convocation on Thursday, April 23, from 9 am to 2:45 pm at Holy Family Parish in Kirkland. Archbishop Etienne will provide guidance for lay ministers, and Deacon Frank DiGirolamo will give insight into Pope Leo’s Dilexi te. This event is for all professional lay ministers, and we encourage all laypeople who work for the Church in ministry and parish administration to attend. Learn more and register here: https://archseattle.org/events/lay-ministry-convocation

From SEAtoday - Food & Drink

Event details

item.title: College Possible Washington Coffee Chat
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: SCERA, 625 S. 4th Street, Renton, WA

Coffee Chat with College Possible Washington Join College Possible Washington for a casual Coffee Chat with Executive Director Lauren Treacy. This informal gathering is an opportunity for local partners, supporters, and community members to connect and learn more about our work supporting students on their path to a college degree. College Possible Washington provides intensive college access and success programming for students from low-income backgrounds and those who are the first in their families to attend college. Through personalized coaching and proven support from high school through college graduation, we help students overcome barriers and achieve their educational goals. We’ll share updates on our new full-time staff model, our three-year strategic plan, and the impact of our programs across the greater Seattle area. Attendees will also have the chance to ask questions and engage in conversation about the organization’s direction and community partnerships. Date: Thursday, April 23rd Time: 11:00 AM – 1:00 PM \(drop-in hours\) Location: Renton Chamber of Commerce To let us know you are coming, please complete this form. We look forward to connecting with you!

From SEAtoday - Food & Drink

Location: The Royal Room, 5000 Rainier Ave South, Seattle, WA

Join us for Live Music Happy Hour with the Joe Casalini Trio. The Casalini Trio has played happy hours at the Royal Room since 2019. They play laid-back, swinging versions of great tunes by Duke Ellington, Billy Strayhorn, Hoagie Carmichael, Phillip Johnston, and other great American songwriters of the last 100 years. The happy hour usually lasts about an hour and a half. Good drinks, good people, good ambience, and there’s never a cover charge, although the band often encourages donations to local food banks and other worthy causes.

From SEAtoday - Food & Drink

item.title: Tour De Pints
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Location ID: 3dbf88ff-8af6-4e33-ad12-3f9f8d99e0b6

Join the throng of around 100 riders on a looping route through North Seattle neighborhoods - Fremont, Ballard, Greenwood, Ravena, and more. The route is still a secret but will be announced shortly before the ride!The Tour De Pints is Seattle's biggest bike and brew ride since 2011. We'll take a safe route that avoids busy car roads as much as possible and suitable for all skill levels. You can't avoid North Seattle hills though so be ready for just a bit of that!We'll visit five breweries - some old route favorites (it wouldn't be a Tour De Pints without Flying Bike!) and some new places we've never had on the route before. The route is roughly a 20 mile loop, starting at a different brewery near where it begins!Swag raffles will take place at each brewery with the biggest and best prizes saved for the final location!Sign up at TourDePints.com

From Seattle Beer Week

item.title: Seattle Vs. Olympia Hot Dog Relay Throwdown
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 1:30 PM

Location: Location ID: m9stfl7e

Join us at Halcyon Brewing for our 2nd annual Hot Dog Relay Throwdown as we pit 3 Seattle breweries (Halcyon, Big Time and Ladd & Lass) vs. 3 Olympia breweries (Ilk, 3 Magnets and Well 80. In addition to the brewers competition, consumers can enter the competition with teams of 3. The winners will receive a be-jewelled belt a multitude of schwag and gift cards. Competition format: Teams of 3 and each person needs to consume one hot dog and drink one pint of beer before the next person goes. The fastest team wins.

From Seattle Beer Week

item.title: Pine Box Beer Can Derby
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Location ID: m7279q0m

The race is on! Come & try your luck racing cans cub scout style down the tracks to beer glory. Join us at 3pm to purchase a beer, canned of course, & a race kit. We’ll race in heats starting at 5pm w/ prizes for 1st, 2nd, & 3rd + most attractive car! Tools will be provided. Cub scout gear encouraged! See you at the starting line.3pm to 5pm - Build Time 5pm - RACE TIME!!ALL CARS MUST PASS INSPECTION TO QUALIFY FOR THE RACE Following are the inspection points 1. The can weight shall not exceed 5.0 ounces. The official race scale shall be considered final. 2. The overall length of the can shall not exceed 7 inches. 3. The overall width of the can shall not exceed 2 ¾ inches. 4. The can must have 1 ¾” clearance between the wheels. 5. The can must have 3/8” clearance underneath the body. 6. The can may be shaped any way that is desired. 7. The can must be freewheeling, with no starting devices. 8. You must use official Pine Box Wheels and Axles, YOUR CAN CAR MUST BE BUILT ON SITE.Each can must pass inspection by the official inspection committee  before it will be allowed to compete. The Inspection Committee has the  responsibility to disqualify those cans that do not meet these  specifications.Hosted by New Belgium Brewing

From Seattle Beer Week

item.title: Sarah Bruhner @ Perihelion Brewery
item.description:
Tuesday, June 9, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Perihelion Brewery, 2800 16th Ave South, Seattle, WA 98144

Michigan native, now Seattle-based, Sarah Brunner is a full time musician, both solo and with her band The Hipocrats. With influences ranging from Brandi Carlile, to Sam Smith to The Lumineers, she uses rhythm guitar just as much as a vocal melody to define a song.We are excited to have Sarah at Perihelion from 7-9pm for some lovely live music.

From Perihelion Brewery

item.title: Mortgage Lifters @ Perihelion Brewery
item.description:
Tuesday, September 22, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Perihelion Brewery, 2800 16th Ave S, Seattle

Seattle and Vashon Island based band playing original and classic swing pop songs. Kim Thal, Robert Hinrix, Johnny Calcagno, and Tony Mates. Also known in some circles as Radiator Charlie's Mortgage Lifters. Tunes of the Golden Era.
